title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Feast
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Lamb Ouzi with our authentic whole slow roasted recipe. A traditional Middle Eastern feast, perfect for special occasions. Get the full recipe here!
longTailKeywords: how to make Lamb Ouzi whole slow roasted
lowCompetitionKeywords: homemade whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi
traditional Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Lamb Ouzi: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Feast
h2Headings: Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
Pro Tips for the Best Slow Roasted Lamb
seo.imageAlt: A whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi, a traditional Middle Eastern dish, garnished and ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich flavors of a traditional Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi, slow roasted to tender perfection.

description: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted is a majestic centerpiece for any Middle Eastern feast, featuring a whole lamb or large cut, traditionally stuffed with aromatic spiced rice and nuts. This impressive dish is slow roasted to achieve incredibly tender, fall-off-the-bone meat infused with rich, savory flavors. It is a true culinary celebration, perfect for gatherings and special occasions.

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb Marinade
instructions.text: In a bowl, combine olive oil, minced garlic, lemon juice, seven spice, paprika, cumin, turmeric, salt, black pepper, and yogurt. Mix well to form a paste.

instructions.title: Marinate the Lamb
instructions.text: Pat the butterflied lamb dry. Rub the marinade generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into all crevices. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, for best flavor.

instructions.title: Prepare the Rice Stuffing
instructions.text: Heat ghee in a large pot. Saute chopped onion until translucent, then add ground lamb or beef and cook until browned. Drain excess fat. Add soaked basmati rice, seven spice, cinnamon, cardamom, salt, and pepper. Stir well. Pour in chicken or beef broth, bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes until rice is partially cooked and liquid is absorbed. Stir in toasted pine nuts, almonds, and raisins if using.

instructions.title: Stuff and Roll the Lamb
instructions.text: Lay the marinated lamb flat on a large roasting pan lined with foil or parchment. Spread the partially cooked rice stuffing evenly over the lamb, leaving a border. Carefully roll the lamb into a compact roast and tie it securely with butchers twine at several intervals to hold its shape.

instructions.title: Slow Roast the Lamb
instructions.text: Preheat oven to 160C (325F). Cover the roasting pan tightly with foil. Roast for 3.5 to 4 hours, or until the lamb is fork-tender. Remove foil for the last 30-45 minutes to allow the lamb to brown and crisp up slightly. The internal temperature should reach 70C (160F) for medium or higher for well-done.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven and let it rest, covered, for at least 20-30 minutes before carving. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and moist roast. Garnish with fresh chopped parsley or mint, toasted nuts, and pomegranate seeds before serving.

tips: For an even richer flavor, marinate the lamb for a full 24 hours, allowing the spices to deeply penetrate the meat.
If a whole lamb is too daunting, use a large bone-in or boneless lamb leg or shoulder. Adjust cooking time accordingly.
Serve Lamb Ouzi with a side of cool yogurt sauce or a refreshing cucumber and tomato salad to balance the rich flavors.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of the Middle East, where food is not just sustenance, but an expression of love, hospitality, and celebration. Among the pantheon of grand Middle Eastern dishes, few command as much respect and admiration as Lamb Ouzi. This magnificent creation, often the centerpiece of festive gatherings, transforms humble ingredients into an unforgettable feast. Imagine succulent, fall-off-the-bone lamb, slow-roasted to perfection, nestled on a bed of fragrant, spiced rice studded with toasted nuts and dried fruits. This isn't just a meal; it's an experience, a true showstopper that promises to delight every palate.

## What is Lamb Ouzi?

Lamb Ouzi, sometimes spelled "Ozzie" or "Ozi," is a traditional Middle Eastern dish renowned for its impressive presentation and exquisite flavors. While variations exist across the region, the essence remains the same: tender, aromatic lamb served with a richly flavored rice pilaf. The "whole slow roasted" aspect elevates it to a truly spectacular dish, typically featuring a whole lamb leg or shoulder, meticulously seasoned and cooked until unbelievably tender. Unlike some versions where the lamb is stewed or cooked with the rice, this particular rendition focuses on a standalone slow roast, allowing the meat to develop a deep, caramelized crust while remaining incredibly juicy within. It’s a dish synonymous with special occasions, family gatherings, and holidays like Eid, where its grandeur makes a statement of generosity and warmth. The magic of Lamb Ouzi lies in this slow cooking process, which breaks down the meat fibers, infusing them with spices and rendering them melt-in-your-mouth tender.

## Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Crafting this majestic Lamb Ouzi requires a blend of quality ingredients and aromatic spices. Here’s what you’ll need for a whole lamb leg (about 5-7 pounds) and its accompanying rice:

For the Lamb:
*   1 whole lamb leg or shoulder (bone-in preferred for flavor)
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt (full-fat)
*   4-6 cloves garlic, minced
*   1/4 cup olive oil
*   2 tbsp Middle Eastern Seven Spice blend
*   1 tbsp ground cumin
*   1 tbsp ground coriander
*   1 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp black pepper
*   2 tsp salt (or to taste)
*   Juice of 1 lemon
*   Optional: Fresh rosemary or thyme sprigs

For the Aromatic Rice:
*   3 cups Basmati rice, rinsed
*   4-5 cups lamb or chicken broth (or water)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1/2 cup mixed nuts (pine nuts, slivered almonds, cashews), toasted
*   1/4 cup dried cranberries or raisins (optional)
*   2 tbsp olive oil or ghee
*   1 tbsp Middle Eastern Seven Spice blend
*   1 tsp ground cinnamon
*   1/2 tsp ground cardamom
*   Salt and black pepper to taste

For Garnish:
*   Fresh parsley or mint, chopped
*   Toasted nuts (extra pine nuts, almonds)

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Lamb Ouzi

Preparing a whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi is a labor of love, but the steps are straightforward and the results are incredibly rewarding.

1.  **Prepare the Lamb Marinade:** In a large bowl, combine yogurt, minced garlic, olive oil, Seven Spice blend, cumin, coriander, turmeric, black pepper, salt, and lemon juice. Mix well to form a thick paste.
2.  **Marinate the Lamb:** Using a sharp knife, score the lamb leg deeply all over (about 1 inch deep) to allow the marinade to penetrate. Rub the marinade generously over the entire lamb leg, ensuring it gets into the scores. For best results, place the lamb in a large roasting pan, cover it, and refrigerate for at least 8 hours, or preferably overnight.
3.  **Preheat Oven & Roast:** Take the lamb out of the fridge 1 hour before roasting to bring it closer to room temperature. Preheat your oven to 300°F (150°C). Add 1-2 cups of water or broth to the bottom of the roasting pan. Cover the pan tightly with foil or a lid.
4.  **Slow Roast:** Roast the lamb for 4-6 hours, or until it is fork-tender and easily pulls away from the bone. The exact time will depend on the size of your lamb leg. For the last 30-45 minutes, remove the foil to allow the lamb to brown and develop a beautiful crust. Baste occasionally with the pan juices.
5.  **Rest the Lamb:** Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven, transfer it to a carving board, and tent it loosely with foil. Let it rest for at least 20-30 minutes. This is crucial for the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and moist roast.
6.  **Prepare the Aromatic Rice:** While the lamb is resting, prepare the rice. Heat olive oil or ghee in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Sauté the chopped onion until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the rinsed Basmati rice, Seven Spice blend, cinnamon, and cardamom. Stir well to coat the rice grains.
7.  **Cook the Rice:** Pour in the lamb or chicken broth. Add salt and pepper to taste. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender. Stir in the toasted nuts and dried fruits (if using) after the rice is cooked and fluffed.
8.  **Assemble and Serve:** On a large serving platter, create a bed of the aromatic rice. Place the whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi prominently on top of the rice. Garnish generously with fresh chopped parsley or mint and additional toasted nuts. Serve immediately.

## Pro Tips for the Best Slow Roasted Lamb

Achieving the perfect Lamb Ouzi requires attention to a few key details that can elevate your dish from good to extraordinary.

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marinating time. The longer the lamb sits in the marinade, the more flavor it will absorb, resulting in a deeply aromatic and tender roast. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Low and Slow:** The secret to truly tender, fall-off-the-bone lamb is low-temperature, slow roasting. Resist the urge to crank up the heat; patience will be rewarded with unparalleled succulence.
*   **Resting Period:** Never skip resting the meat. This allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb the juices, ensuring a moist and flavorful roast. Carving too soon will result in dry meat.
*   **Don't Overcook the Rice:** Basmati rice cooks quickly. Follow the liquid-to-rice ratio carefully and avoid lifting the lid too often during cooking. Fluff with a fork once done.
*   **Toasting Nuts:** Toasting the nuts before adding them to the rice or using them as garnish brings out their natural oils and enhances their flavor and crunch. Do this gently in a dry pan or a little oil.
*   **Quality Ingredients:** Start with a good quality lamb leg or shoulder. The better the meat, the better the final dish. Fresh spices also make a noticeable difference.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Lamb Ouzi is a complete meal in itself, but it can be beautifully complemented by a few fresh sides to balance its richness.

*   **Fresh Salads:** A crisp, tangy salad like Fattoush (with toasted pita bread, vegetables, and sumac dressing) or Tabbouleh (a vibrant parsley and bulgur salad) provides a refreshing contrast.
*   **Yogurt Sauce:** A simple cucumber and mint yogurt sauce (tzatziki or laban bi khiar) can be served on the side for a cooling element.
*   **Vegetables:** Steamed green beans with a hint of lemon or roasted asparagus can add a touch of green to your plate.
*   **Pickles:** Assorted Middle Eastern pickles (torshi) offer a delightful sour counterpoint.
*   **Drinks:** Pair with a refreshing mint lemonade, a dry red wine, or simply sparkling water.

## Variations, Substitutions & FAQs

**Variations:**
*   **Spices:** Feel free to adjust the spice blend to your preference. Some recipes might include a touch of smoked paprika or a pinch of chili for a subtle kick.
*   **Rice Add-ins:** You can add sautéed carrots, peas, or chickpeas to the rice for extra texture and nutrition.
*   **Smaller Portions:** If a whole leg is too much, you can use lamb shanks or a smaller lamb shoulder cut. Adjust roasting time accordingly.

**Substitutions:**
*   **Nuts:** If you don't have mixed nuts, use your favorite single type, like just almonds or pistachios.
*   **Dried Fruit:** Dates, apricots, or even golden raisins can be used instead of cranberries or regular raisins.
*   **Broth:** If you don't have lamb or chicken broth, water can be used for the rice, though the flavor will be less rich.
*   **Yogurt:** While yogurt helps tenderize, if you don't have it, you can use a combination of lemon juice and olive oil as the base for your marinade.

**FAQs:**
*   **Can I make Lamb Ouzi ahead of time?**
    Yes, you can marinate the lamb up to 2 days in advance. The rice can be cooked a few hours ahead and gently reheated. It's best to roast the lamb on the day of serving for optimal freshness and tenderness.
*   **How do I store leftovers?**
    Store leftover lamb and rice separately in airtight containers in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.
*   **How do I reheat leftovers?**
    Gently reheat the lamb covered in the oven at a low temperature (around 275°F/135°C) with a splash of broth to prevent drying out. Reheat the rice in a microwave or a covered pot on the stovetop with a tablespoon of water, fluffing occasionally.
*   **What if I don't have Middle Eastern Seven Spice?**
    You can create a close approximation by mixing equal parts ground allspice, black pepper, cinnamon, cloves, coriander, cumin, and nutmeg.

Lamb Ouzi is more than just a meal; it's a celebration, a testament to the rich culinary heritage of the Middle East. With its tender lamb, fragrant rice, and festive presentation, it promises to be the crowning glory of your next dinner party or family feast. Don't be intimidated by its grandeur; follow these steps, and you'll create a dish that will be remembered and savored by all.
